title: salix retusa var. multiflora
intro: Salix retusa var. multiflora, also known as the dwarf willow or multi-flowered dwarf willow, is a shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family and is native to northern Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The medicinal properties of Salix retusa var. multiflora also contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, salve, or infusion, making it a versatile remedy in traditional folk medicine.
